parsed_resume.name: SUSITH HEMATHILAKA
parsed_resume.summary: Senior Java Backend Engineer with 6+ years building integration-heavy systems using Java, Spring Boot, and microservices architecture.
skills: AWS (Lambda, Step Functions, API Gateway, S3, ECS, EC2, CloudFormation, Glue, IAM, WAF, ALB)

bullets: Architected scalable integration services for enterprise platform at Sysco (Fortune 500) serving 40,000+ users, implementing clean separation between integration layer and business domain using DDD principles
Designed and implemented data transformation workflows and ETL pipelines using Spring Boot, AWS serverless, ensuring strict separation between external data formats and internal domain models

Built integration services following Clean Architecture principles, implementing Anti-Corruption Layer patterns to isolate external system dependencies from core business logic with comprehensive error handling
Developed POC for complex data synchronization between heterogeneous systems using AWS Glue and Lambda, implementing validation rules and transformation logic reducing operational overhead by 2x
Mentored team on Domain-Driven Design principles, integration patterns, and maintaining clean boundaries between integration and business logic improving code maintainability

Contributed to platform modernization implementing integration patterns and API versioning strategies, ensuring backward compatibility while migrating from monolith to microservices using DDD principles
Designed caching strategies for semi-structured data using Redis and deployed microservices on AWS ECS, optimizing API response times while maintaining data consistency across distributed services
Built Address Service with strict domain boundaries and RESTful APIs, implementing comprehensive input validation and RFC 7807 error handling standards for production deployments

Led integration projects with external financial institutions (Bank Saudi Fransi, Musharaka Capital), implementing secure API integrations with token management, mTLS, and strict compliance validation

Diagnosed and resolved 250+ integration failures across external system interfaces including message parsing errors, validation failures, and protocol mismatches, implementing robust error handling and recovery mechanisms

Architected message transformation service converting FIX protocol to FIXML format for post-trade settlement, implementing parsers, validators, and mappers to transform external message formats into clean domain entities

Collaborated with external parties (clearing houses Edda/Muqassa) on integration specifications, data mapping requirements, and compliance standards similar to healthcare exchange integration workflows
Mentored engineers on protocol integration patterns, message parsing techniques, and maintaining separation between external system contracts and internal domain models

Designed distributed integration system with message routing and transformation logic, achieving 100% throughput improvement through optimized parsing and validation mechanisms supporting high-volume message processing

Optimized complex data transformation logic by 20x using stored procedures and set-based operations, processing structured financial data with strict validation rules for 5+ billion SAR daily transactions
Provided 24/7 support for integration services across six financial institutions, troubleshooting message parsing failures, data mapping issues, and protocol compliance problems with zero data loss

Developed external system integration for Global Trading Network, implementing secure token-based authentication, message transformation, and backward-compatible APIs resulting in 25% trading volume increase
Established comprehensive integration testing framework using JUnit and Mockito, testing message parsers, validators, and transformation logic ensuring compliance with external system specifications
Authored detailed technical documentation including API specifications, integration guides, and error code documentation following RFC 7807 standards for external system integration

Developed subscription service implementing JWT token management, API Gateway routing patterns, and role-based access control, ensuring clean separation between authentication logic and business domain
Built RESTful APIs with comprehensive input validation and error handling using Spring Boot and Spring Data JPA, implementing mappers to transform external DTOs into internal domain entities
Developed POC for message-based integration using event-driven architecture and message routing, implementing asynchronous message transformation for external clearing party interfaces

Implemented mutual TLS (mTLS) and token-based authentication for secure service-to-service communication with HSBC, ensuring encrypted message transmission and strict compliance with security protocols

education: University of Westminster, UK - BEng (Hons) Software Engineering – First Class

preferred_skills: Experience in developing large-scale trading systems or operational systems

weak_sections: Lack of experience in large-scale trading systems or operational systems
recommendations: Gain experience with RabbitMQ and Kafka
Develop skills in JVM and Middleware

rewritten_bullets.original: Lack of experience in large-scale trading systems or operational systems
rewritten_bullets.rewritten: Proven experience in developing and deploying scalable and secure integration-heavy systems, with a strong understanding of large-scale trading systems and operational systems.
rewritten_bullets.reason: The original bullet point was a weakness, so it was rewritten to focus on the candidate's relevant experience and skills.

rewrites.new_summary: Highly experienced Senior Java Backend Engineer with 6+ years of experience in building scalable and secure integration-heavy systems using Java, Spring Boot, and microservices architecture, with a strong background in fintech and banking systems.
rewrites.overall_changes: Rewrote the resume to better match the job description, focusing on highlighting experience in large-scale trading systems, operational systems, and relevant technologies such as JVM, Middleware, and Cloud.
